summary: Choosing the Right Graph (28 minute read) RDF/OWL is better for governed, interoperable knowledge with formal meaning, reasoning, provenance, and linked-data publishing. summary: On the dl (7 minute read) The `dl` HTML element, along with `dt` for terms and `dd` for details, provides a semantic way to mark up lists of name-value pairs in HTML. This structure allows for one term to have multiple values or for pairs to be wrapped in a `div` for easier styling. Using these semantic tags instead of generic containers helps assistive technologies like screen readers provide more context and navigation options to users.

title: How to compete when you can't outshine the giants
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-26, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fconversion-rate-experts.com%2Fhow-to-compete-when-you-cant-outshine-the-giants-copywriting-friday%2F%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e63f67f86-ee4aa17b-2236-4510-ac2e-32bd69ce842a-000000/7HMS7VjsjDrztswIJdqwXHl3cwnEzPF36iCUHAlhBeg=452
summary: How to compete when you can't outshine the giants (6 minute read) A small soap brand beat giant competitors by doing the opposite of category marketing. Instead of miracle claims, it told the truth, mocked fake ingredients, and admitted limitations upfront. That honesty reduced skepticism, so its simple, specific claims felt more credible. The core lesson is positioning first, then copy. Stop adding hype, define what you won't claim, and let plain language do the selling.

title: The Social Contract of Writing
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-26, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fjola.dev%2Fposts%2Fthe-social-contract-of-writing%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e63f67f86-ee4aa17b-2236-4510-ac2e-32bd69ce842a-000000/NtMZtP-n1_nicrJSuCoDq1YT5ptlXeof2Pha1uCIND4=452
summary: The Social Contract of Writing (4 minute read) LLMs are flooding the internet with competent but repetitive writing, creating a growing backlash against low-effort content. AI-written text breaks an implicit contract where writers are expected to invest more effort than readers. Even good content feels empty if that effort isn't there. As AI text spreads, originality and human expression become more valuable, even over polish or correctness.

title: The Revenge of The Measurers
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-26, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.hackyexperiments.com%2Fblog%2Fthe-revenge-of-the-measurers%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e63f67f86-ee4aa17b-2236-4510-ac2e-32bd69ce842a-000000/sCd_qT6JTKoU4YRsMXPTRQRdPuQXN3hnm_zZrEthTX0=452
summary: The Revenge of The Measurers (10 minute read) Cloudflare says its first mass layoff targeted “measurers”, the coordination-heavy roles created by headcount-driven growth. AI can now collapse this overhead while preserving real product complexity. Companies move toward smaller teams of producers and marketers, with AI increasing the output gap between top and average performers. Founder-led firms move first, forcing others to follow, while startups benefit most by starting lean.

title: Deleting a Google API Key Doesn't Revoke It for Up to 23 Minutes
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-26, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.aikido.dev%2Fblog%2Fgoogle-api-keys-deletion%3Futm_source=tldrit/1/0100019e6446c88e-c9eeebee-a7b0-4d21-89b5-feb0ce39d632-000000/8h3IdM55czkZE8csoeK7UZ17Moi0hpI9wfFRhR9Q_Gw=452
summary: Deleting a Google API Key Doesn't Revoke It for Up to 23 Minutes (2 minute read) Security researchers found that deleted Google API keys can continue working for up to 23 minutes, creating a dangerous revocation window where attackers can still make API calls, access enabled services, or rack up charges. Until Google changes this behavior, teams should rotate or disable exposed keys immediately and treat deletion as a delayed cleanup step rather than an instant kill switch.

summary: Introducing BenchBench (5 minute read) BenchBench is a benchmark that tests how well models can create a benchmark. It works as a great benchmark for model abilities as well as a test of models' self-awareness. The benchmark tests creativity and not just problem-solving ability. In tests, GPT 5.2 was the only winner, with every other model, from Opus 4.6 to GPT 5.5, struggling to create an actually useful benchmark that others had a hard time solving.

title: Staged publishing and new install-time controls for npm
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-27, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgithub.blog%2Fchangelog%2F2026-05-22-staged-publishing-and-new-install-time-controls-for-npm%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019e691d8006-70166313-b870-436d-a525-ab69409b7f43-000000/cQwL874LGZPmoRv8YLzfn_6yaITPw-Be74nHUjCTGB8=452
summary: Staged publishing and new install-time controls for npm (2 minute read) GitHub introduced staged publishing and install-time controls for npm packages, giving maintainers safer ways to roll out package releases and giving consumers more control over what gets installed. The changes are a direct response to the growing risk of compromised packages and maintainer accounts, making npm supply-chain defense more operational instead of relying only on fast takedowns after a bad release spreads.

title: Introducing Nova, Dropbox's internal platform for coding agents
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-27, Reading time: 7 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fdropbox.tech%2Fmachine-learning%2Fintroducing-nova-our-internal-platform-for-coding-agents%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019e691d8006-70166313-b870-436d-a525-ab69409b7f43-000000/2sWWhpSpkMpvAa2sjYJRm3xBVdn6R1tozzBkqjRx9Dg=452
summary: Introducing Nova, Dropbox's internal platform for coding agents (7 minute read) Nova is an internal cloud platform within Dropbox for running coding agents across its engineering workflows. It lets engineers run multiple coding sessions in parallel, execute agents inside isolated environments pinned to specific repository commits, validate proposed changes with Bazel-backed build and test commands, and continue sessions when validation fails.

title: Agent Memory: An Anatomy
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-27, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FypmLVX/1/0100019e691d8006-70166313-b870-436d-a525-ab69409b7f43-000000/HW2M03d8cAys7p7nRAMkarZzzhTQg7pB5_WzyrHYeao=452
summary: Agent Memory: An Anatomy (9 minute read) Agent memory systems are better understood as a pipeline of extraction, storage, and retrieval rather than a single magical “memory” feature. Agent libraries compress conversations into facts, store them in vectors/tables/graphs, retrieve them later, and struggle with contradictions, stale context, procedural memory, and future intentions.

title: cmux
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-05-27, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fmanaflow-ai%2Fcmux%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019e691d8006-70166313-b870-436d-a525-ab69409b7f43-000000/VWYs_VTgIl1x-ASSmcc0-g41B6KgxbPbxmXSz-PlZiw=452
summary: cmux (GitHub Repo) cmux is a new open-source macOS terminal app built on Ghostty that adds vertical tabs, contextual notifications, and an integrated scriptable browser specifically designed for managing multiple AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex in parallel. The native Swift/AppKit app features session restoration, a sidebar showing git branches and PR status for each workspace, and a CLI for automation—all while maintaining compatibility with existing Ghostty configurations.
